entries.definition:
b-r-h-n (the quadruple structure of this root together with
the limited derivatives it has in Arabic give support to its being a
very early borrowing, possibly from Persian. Some philologists,
however, consider it a derivation from root ب/ر/ه b-r-h ‘to cut’ or
‘whiteness’). Of this root, two forms occur eight times in the
Qur’an: بُرْهَان burhān seven times and بُرْهَانَان burhānān once.

بُرْهَان burhān [n., dual بُرْهَانَان burhānān] proof, evidence (27:64)
هَاتُوا بُرْهَانَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ produce your evidence, if you are truthful.

ٱلْبَرِيَّة ʾal-bariyyatu (see ب/ر/أ b-r-ʾ).
entries.definition_english:
b-r-h-n (the quadruple structure of this root together with
the limited derivatives it has in Arabic give support to its being a
very early borrowing, possibly from Persian. Some philologists,
however, consider it a derivation from root ب/ر/ه b-r-h ‘to cut’ or
‘whiteness’). Of this root, two forms occur eight times in the
Qur’an: بُرْهَان burhān seven times and بُرْهَانَان burhānān once.

بُرْهَان burhān [n., dual بُرْهَانَان burhānān] proof, evidence (27:64)
هَاتُوا بُرْهَانَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صَادِقِينَ produce your evidence, if you are truthful.

ٱلْبَرِيَّة ʾal-bariyyatu (see ب/ر/أ b-r-ʾ).
